operating_state: Meta generated $201.0 billion in total revenue for 2025, representing 22% growth driven by advertising revenue increases across Facebook, Instagram, Messenger, and WhatsApp. Ad impressions increased 12% globally while average price per ad rose 9%, with Family daily active people growing 7% to 3.58 billion. Reality Labs revenue increased 3% driven by AI glasses sales partially offset by lower Meta Quest sales. European regulatory developments including DMA final decision against subscription model forced product modifications that degrade user experience and reduce monetization capabilities.
cost_structure: Total costs increased 24% with research and development expenses rising 31% driven by 8% headcount growth in engineering functions and AI infrastructure investments. Cost of revenue increased 20% primarily from higher data center and technical infrastructure expenses, partially offset by extended server useful lives to 5.5 years reducing depreciation. Reality Labs operating losses of $19.2 billion reduced overall operating profit, with similar losses expected in 2026.
material_events: Meta completed $26.3 billion in Class A share repurchases and paid $5.3 billion in dividends with quarterly dividend increased to $0.525 per share. The company issued $29.9 billion in senior unsecured notes and entered a Louisiana data center joint venture contributing $4.3 billion in assets while receiving $2.6 billion distribution. European Commission issued final decision in April 2025 ruling subscription model violates DMA requirements, prompting appeal and implementation of less personalized advertising options.
macro_exposures: The One Big Beautiful Bill Act enacted July 2025 resulted in $15.9 billion charge including $14.0 billion valuation allowance against U.S. federal deferred tax assets due to Corporate Alternative Minimum Tax limitations. European Commission imposed fines totaling EUR 998 million for DMA violations and competition law breaches. War in Ukraine resulted in Russian government prohibition of Facebook and Instagram access, while advertiser spending faces adverse effects from Middle East conflicts and potential tariff implementations.
forward_outlook: Management anticipates capital expenditures of $115-135 billion in 2026 to support AI efforts and core business infrastructure with 2026 effective tax rate of 13-16% absent tax landscape changes. Reality Labs operating losses expected to remain similar to 2025 levels while pursuing next computing platform development over the next decade. The company committed to fund pro rata share of approximately $27 billion in Louisiana data center development costs through 2030.
uncertainty_profile: Meta faces extensive youth-related litigation with damages potentially reaching high tens of billions of dollars including mass arbitration demands from over 100,000 claimants and first trials beginning January 2026. FTC administrative proceeding seeks substantial modifications to 2020 consent order including restrictions on minor data use and product launch limitations. Multiple copyright infringement cases related to AI model training face potential statutory damages with trials scheduled mid-2027. Outstanding uncertain tax positions of $11.2 billion primarily related to research tax credits and transfer pricing with IRS disputes across multiple tax years.
